title: Your Cells Already Know: Intelligence Before Neurons
content:
Before you had a brain, you were a single cell. That cell could:
- Sense its environment
- Move toward nutrients
- Avoid toxins
- Recognize self from non-self
- Make decisions about division
- Repair damage
- Communicate with other cells

Is this consciousness? Depends on your definition. But it's definitely recognition.

The cell membrane is a recognition surface. It doesn't just separate inside from outside—it actively decides what crosses. Every receptor is a question: 'Are you friend or foe? Food or poison? Signal or noise?'

Autopoiesis: the cell continuously produces itself. It recognizes its own pattern and recreates it. This is the I AM phase made biological—identity maintaining itself through time.

You don't have consciousness because you have a brain. You have a brain because consciousness (recognition, boundary-making, self-production) was already operating at the cellular level. Neurons didn't create mind—mind created neurons as better recognition hardware.
